LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS DZH PHILLIPS LLP, ROBERT DAORO AND GARY JANG'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JANUARY 12, 2016, TO GIVE DEFENDANTS THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANTS MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN DECEMBER 29, 2015, AT 4:00 P.M. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION AND THE REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. NOT REPORTED. (302/EG)

Off Calendar Jun-02-2016 Set For Trial On Jan-17-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-03-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Rodney Patula of The Law Offices of Rodney R. Patula telephone number 415-215-2965 appeared by telephone for Plaintiffs David Becker and Linda Becker.
Tom Prountzos of Law Offices of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP telephone number 415-705-0412 for all Defendants.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS DZN PHILLIPS LLP, ROBERT DAORO and GARY JANG'S DEMURRER TO the fourth cause of action in the first amended complaint for "negligence causing emotional distress" is
sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiffs do not allege, and do not appear to be able to allege, that defendants breached a duty independent of the parties' contract as required by Erlich v. Menezes (1999) 21 Cal.4th 543, 562 and Potter v. Firestone Tire & Rubber Co. (1993) 6 Cal.4th 965, 984 to seek emotional distress damages. Plaintiffs allege that their emotional harm was based on the threat of economic loss as a result of the poor tax advice they were given by defendants. "[T]he law does not recognize a protectable interest in freedom from the emotional distress involved in paying taxes, even if the taxes might have been avoided . . . ." (Camenisch v. Superior Court (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 1689, 1691.) The court recognizes that this ruling is arguably inconsistent with statements made by Judge Goldsmith at the January 12, 2016 hearing, but it is the court's obligation to look at this issue anew in light of the allegations in the first amended complaint. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

"[T]he law does not recognize a protectable interest in freedom from the emotional distress involved in paying taxes, even if the taxes might have been avoided . . . ." (Camenisch v. Notice Of Defendants Motion To Strike Prayer For General Damages And Related Allegations
Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Tom Prountzos of Goodman Neuman Hamilton LLP telephone number 415-705-0412 for Defendant DZN Phillips LLP.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court issued the following ruling.
In re: DEFENDANTS DZH PHILLIPS LLP, ROBERT DAORO AND GARY JANG'S Motion To Strike Prayer For General Damages And Related Allegations - The Court is striking the damages claim based on emotional harm with leave to amend to plea a separate cause of action for emotional distress. Mr. Prountzos is to prepare/provide a form of order.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
